title: Why Creativity Must Be Taught in Every Class
subtitle: 学校正在扼杀创造力：为什么必须改变

paragraphs.en: You can't see it, smell it, or hear it, and people disagree on how to define it or where it comes from. It isn't a school subject, but it can be learned. It is a quality required of artists, but also present in the lives of scientists and entrepreneurs. All of us benefit from it and we thrive when we wield it.
paragraphs.zh: 你看不见、闻不到、也听不见它，人们对如何定义它或它从何而来争论不休。它不是一门学校科目，却可以习得。它是艺术家必备的品质，但也存在于科学家和企业家的生活中。我们所有人都从中受益，当我们运用它时便能蓬勃发展。

words.meaning: v. 蓬勃发展，茁壮成长

paragraphs.en: It is a delicate thing, easily stamped out; it flourishes most when people are playful and childlike. Meanwhile, it works best with deep knowledge and expertise. This mysterious but teachable quality is creativity, the subject of a report by the Durham Commission on Creativity and Education.
paragraphs.zh: 它是一种脆弱的东西，容易被扼杀；当人们像孩子般玩耍时它最为繁荣。同时，它与深厚的知识和专业技能结合时效果最佳。这种神秘但可教的品质就是创造力，也是达勒姆创造力与教育委员会一份报告的主题。

paragraphs.en: The report concludes that creativity should not only relate to drama, music and art, but that creative thinking ought to run through all of school life, infusing the way humanities and sciences are learned. When children are regarded as vessels to be filled with facts, creativity does not prosper; nor does it when teachers' sole objective is coaching towards exams.
paragraphs.zh: 报告得出结论，创造力不应只与戏剧、音乐和艺术相关，创造性思维应贯穿整个学校生活，融入人文和科学的学习方式。当孩子被视为装填事实的容器时，创造力便无法繁荣；当教师的唯一目标是应试辅导时亦是如此。

paragraphs.en: One suggestion is a network of teacher-led creativity collaboratives to support teaching for creativity across the curriculum. The value placed on arts by the independent education sector is clear — one only has to look at the remarkable arts facilities at Britain's top private schools.
paragraphs.zh: 一项建议是建立教师主导的创造力协作网络，支持跨课程的创造力教学。私立教育对艺术的重视显而易见——只需看看英国顶尖私立学校卓越的艺术设施便知。

words.meaning: n. 协作组织，合作机构

paragraphs.en: But in the state sector, the excessive focus on English, maths and science threatens to crush arts subjects. There has been a 28.1% decline in students taking creative subjects since 2014. This discrepancy between state and private education is a matter of social justice. As lives are affected by challenges like climate crisis and workplace automation, humans will need creative thinking more than ever. For all of our sakes, creativity in education must become a priority.
paragraphs.zh: 但在公立学校，对英语、数学和科学的过度关注正威胁着艺术学科。自2014年以来，选修创意学科的学生人数下降了28.1%。公立与私立教育之间的这种差距是一个社会公正问题。面对气候危机和职场自动化等挑战，人类将比以往更需要创造性思维。为了我们所有人，教育中的创造力必须成为优先事项。
